1.title: Which Decade?

11.content: Now, please select the kind of programme that you're looking for.
11.title: Which '50s Genre?

12.content: So, which genre does the programme you seek come under?
12.title: Which '60s Genre?

13.content: So, choose the genre that the programme you desire comes under, please.
13.title: Which '70s Genre?

131.content: Now then, what is the series called?
131.title: Which Sitcom?

13111.title: Who?

13131.content: Right, now how many giantesses will there be in this sitcom?

14.title: Which '80s Genre?

141.content: Now, let's look at the BBC comedies that were made in the 1980s.
141.title: Comedy

1411.content: So how many giantesses will appear in this WWII comedy?
1411.title: Giantesses of Occupied Frace

14111.content: Will it be Michelle of the resistance?
14111.title: Michelle Dubois?

14113.content: Maybe there'll be a trio?
14113.title: Three?

1412.content: So how many female characters from the (in)famous holiday camp will become giantesses?
1412.title: How Many?

14121.content: Which character will become a giantess?
14121.title: Who?

141212.content: Maybe Peggy will be a giantess.
141212.title: Peggy?

1412121.content: Will Peggy grow or will those around her shrink?
1412121.title: How?

1412122.content: Or will it be Sylvia?
1412122.title: Sylvia?!?

14123.content: Would you like to see three of them as giantesses?
14123.title: Three?

1413.content: This is the only remaining sitcom I can think of that has good GTS potential in it that was made in the 1980s.
1413.title: You Rang, M'Lord?

14131.content: Now that the series has been decided how many of the female characters are to be giantesses?
14131.title: How Many?

141311.content: One giantess, now which character will it be? How's about Ivy Stokes/Teasdale?
141311.title: Who?

1413111.content: So will Ivy grow into a giantess or will those around her shrink?
1413111.title: Growth or Shrinking?

14131112.content:
Note: This is set just after Requirem for a Parrot.

Poetic justice is an unusual concept; those who seek it never actually perpetrate it and it never seems to deliver itself in the most brunt fashion but, under the right conditions, it can be a wonderful thing.

In the House of Meldrum the day seemed just like any other. Outside horse-drawn taxis trundled by, Lady Lavender continued to pine for her lost parrot and everyone else just went about their own sweet ways or not-so-sweet ways in some cases. However for each and every one of them a strange force descending over the house would change their lives forever.

Poppy Meldrum was in her room going through her wardrobe in a futile effort to choose a frock to wear on her night out, even though there were at least three hours to go; far too many garments had been tossed aside as being 'boring'. She was just evaluating her thirteenth frock when the force had descended and, upon dismissing the fourteenth frock, the effects of the strange aura began to take effect; just as she reached for the fifteenth she suddenly found herself in a bizarre and yet strangely familiar space. Confused, irritated and unreasoning she had yet to get to grips with the fact that she had suddenly shrunk to the size of her own thumbnail.

Down below, in the kitchen, Mrs. Lipton, Henry, Stokes, James and PC Wilson were all sitting down to tea with Mabel in the background perpetually washing up when this force came forth; a minute after the aura miniaturised Poppy they all miniaturised simultaneously to the same scale. Ivy was absent as she had just gone up to Lady Lavender's room to deal with a summoning that was a mystery to them all.

Barely half a minute later Lord Meldrum and the so-called 'Honourable' Teddy, while trying to get presedent over the other experienced the effect of the aura; all around the house it would take a while for those affected to realise what had happened.

Ivy had just left Lady Lavender's room after she had recited a poem she hoped to engrave on her parrot's tombstone when the latter fell under this extraordinary spell and now found herself lost in her own beclothes; thusly only two in the house remained regular-size now.

Cissy was the last of the residents to be affected; she had just finished writing a letter to her party's treasurer and had just made for the drinkstand when she became influenced by the force. Now, with nearly everyone who entered the household daily shrunk down to the size of fingernails, they had to come to terms with the weird situation and quick as Ivy would soon notice a conspicuous absence of people in the house.
14131112.title: A Different Curse on the House of Meldrum

141311121.content:
All six of them were staring around with fear and awe at the excessively larger kitchen. Each of them had a different level of intensity in their reaction. Alf Stokes was pulling a face at the sense of discomfort the situation gave, James Twelvetrees was all blown up like a bullfrog as he did what he could to retain his dignity, Blanche Lipton was well-and-truly agog at the size of everything she took for granted in her job, Henry Livingstone was bewildered but kept his sense of humour as he started to speak his mind again, Mabel Wheeler was nearly overcome with shock and Arthur Wilson tried to remember his training with regards to unorthodox situations.

"What happened?" Henry asked. With nearly everyone now under the kitchen table there was a pretty much unobstructed view of the bootboy. James and PC Wilson looked at him with contempted disbelief, Mrs. Lipton hid her face in her hands in similar disbelief while Stokes gave him a look as to suggest that it was a very stupid remark; Mabel, on the other hand, just shrugged her shoulders regarding the cause.

"In case you haven't noticed, Henry," Stokes replied, "Somehow we've all gotten shrunk."

"Don't remind me, Mr. Stokes." Mrs. Lipton told him. "It's too scary to contemplate."

"Well," Wilson remarked as he put his police training into practice, "In drastic times it seems that keeping together is most important. Mabel, come here!" She did so in uncertainty as to what he had in mind. "Whatever happens we must take care to stick together. Remember the old maxim from the war."

"Divide and conquer." James responded. "If we stick together we stand a better chance."
141311121.title: In The Kitchen...

1413111212.content:
While all of this was going on George and Teddy Meldrum were trying to deal with the situation in their own ways.

"Good God!" George exclaimed as he finally realised what had happened. "This can't have happened." In the now long distance to the other side of the library Teddy walked across to where George was in the hopes of being able to talk to one another as they had been doing earlier. It took him two minutes just to get halfway in a brisk stroll.

"Well Goerge," He commented as he reached the desk, "I don't suppose you could get me out to Malaya while we're like this." George frowned at this surprisingly cheery comment.

"This is no time to go on about that." He answered. "How are we going to live now that the house is rediculously large?" The question was rehetorical so Teddy didn't answer but instead responded with.

"Oh, George, think positive. Let's try and make the best of a bad job."

"I wouldn't advise that," He interjected, "After that fiasco at the factory."

"Well, what are we going to do then?" Teddy asked; Lord Meldrum just shrugged his shoulders.
1413111212.title: In The Library...

14131112122.content:
Poppy was totally speechless when it hit her what had happened. Her mind raced with trivial problems that being only a quarter of an inch tall had rather than looking at the more serious issues. The first thing she decided to do was to trek through all the garments that she had tossed about and gripe over the fact that they could no longer fit her; it was a struggle for her microscopic feet to make their way through the landcape of expensive textiles. She would be doing this for several minutes yet.

Lady Lavender, trapped on her bed, was scrambling through the gigantic bedsheets, trying to find a way by which she could reach the bell-pull. In spite of her apparent senility she knew that if she could get Ivy back she'd surely help her. Unfortunately the bell-pull was about six inches away from the edge of the bed and, at her current height, seemed to be about 132 feet away from the edge of the bed and there was no way she'd be able to ring it if she managed to get down to the floor. At one point the tired elderly lady sat down and tried to find a way around the problem.
14131112122.title: Upstairs...

141311121222.content:
Cissy, on the other hand, was looking at this adversity in a whole new light. She figured that if people were smaller they'd need less food and, logically, that would mean prosperity and the end of famine. However she had to consider just how many were currently in the same boat as she was and the only way was to explore the house from this new perspective.

Figuring that there was no other way she waded through the deep pile carpet towards the doors that led to the hall; on the way she marvelled at the size of everything in the room, especially the roaring fire that had flames reached seemingly hundreds of feet into the air.

At one point she heard distinct great footfalls from outside of the drawing room, immediately indicating to her that at least one person in the house had not been shrunk; it was difficult to determine but the sharpness of the footfalls suggested that they belonged to a woman.

By the time Cissy found that she had forgotten something important; the doors were shut and the knobs were far too high for her to reach. For an instant she wished for a gypsy moth to fly around in as it would make someone her size traverse the house much quicker and easier and then stopped this wishful thinking and contemplated the gap under the door, wondering if she could fit through it.
141311121222.title: In the Drawing Room...

1413111212222.content:
Cissy decided that trying to crawl under the door would have too much of a risk factor to it and, instead, decided that she'd take shelter under the desk until she was sure that help was in the same room.

At that time Ivy was just beginning to descend the stairs that led to the kitchen; she was still totally oblivious to anything that seemed out of place. Although the tapping of her shoes against the stairs were quite audible to her the distance and the wall between her and the kitchen meant that her approach was still unnoticed by the six who were still trying to figure out what to do in this new situation.

"I think we must check on his lordship." James commented. "If this suddenly happened to us without warning it could just as easily happen to him."

"His lordship is in the library." Stokes informed him. "It will take us all day just to get up the stairs."

"Why don't we use the dumb waiter?" Henry asked. "It would be faster."

"Don't be daft, Henry." James answered with a clip round the ear.

"Well," Mabel interjected, "If we can't get help and we're not gonna grow back then we might as well set up some form of base camp with ready access to the pantry."

"Watch your tongue, Mabel!" Mrs. Lipton told her, not liking the possibility of anybody helping themselves to her perishables. Just then they all noticed distant thuds; turning to face the door they saw it open swiftly to reveal Ivy, full-size, towering over them like nothing they'd seen before. Identifying Ivy as being at the appropriate size to cope with the house they all decide to...
1413111212222.title: Ivy's Immense Entrance

14131112122221.content:
"Good Lord!" Mrs. Lipton said aloud. "Look at Ivy." As she said that Ivy shut the door behind her and took a few steps towards the table, every footfall creating noticable vibrations in the floor.

"I don't believe this." Stokes commented. "How is it that she's still normal-size?!?"

"I'm not entirely sure, Alf Stokes," James responded, "But perhaps it's because only those of us in the kitchen are in this condition."

"Well," Mrs. Lipton stated as she noticed an important possibility, "I hope Ivy doesn't end up in the same boat as us."

Ivy was looking round the kitchen, noticing a conspicuous absence. She figured that her father (Stokes) and James would most likely be upstairs attending to their services and PC Wilson would be back on his beat but wondered as to the others; the result was that Henry would've been in the pantry and Mabel in the scullery but that still left a blank as to where Mrs. Lipton was.

"Henry?" She called out. "Mabel? Mrs. Lipton?" With each call she moved round the table a few paces.

Underneath the table the volume of Ivy's calls caused Henry to cover his ears and Mabel to flinch. Stokes, James and PC Wilson endured as Ivy's calls were nothing compared to the sounds of exploding shells they had experienced during the war while Mrs. Lipton was totally gobsmacked by the effect seeing and hearing Ivy in her present situation had on her.

"There's only one thing we can do." She told the others. "We'll have to get Ivy to notice us." No one argued as Mrs. Lipton appeared to be talking sense.

Doing a double-take Ivy noticed the tea things on the table; she knew none of them could've gone out as some of the cups weren't even empty. Ivy...
14131112122221.title: Mrs Lipton's Moment of Inspiration

141311121222211.content:
Seeing as how she hadn't had one yet Ivy sat down and commenced to help herself to the tea, totally unaware that the rest of the servants were watching every one of her moves that she could from under the table.

"Now then," Stokes commented, "How are we going to get Ivy's attention from down here?"

"Why don't we just call her?" Henry asked.

"Don't talk nonsense, Henry." James told him. "She won't be able to hear us." Mrs. Lipton continually stared at Ivy's ankles before suggesting.

"If we climb up to her shoulder she's bound to hear us." Hearing this they all looked up as high as they could; due to Ivy's seating position they could see no higher than her waist, and that appeared to be 803ft up alone. Ivy was like a mountain goddess to them if they did not follow an Abrahamic faith (i.e. Christian), so instead they compared her to a mountain in general.

"I wouldn't fancy our chances if she starts moving." PC Wilson indicated.

"Needs must." Said James. "There seems to be no other way." With that he approached the nearest of Ivy's shoes while everyone stared at him in shock as to the blunt way he dealt with the matter; Mrs. Lipton shortly followed, seeing as how it was her idea, and Mabel came shortly behind her, seeing as how she was a fairly strong woman. While they clmbed onto Ivy's left shoe PC Wilson strode towards Ivy's right with Alf Stokes close behind and Henry, regarding the situation for a moment, brought up the rear.

Of course Ivy would have to get up at some point; the question is, how far up her are they when she moves.
141311121222211.title: Climb Mt. Teasdale

1413111212222112.content: Having finished her cup Ivy helped herself to yet another one; her leaning over gave those climbing her legs a slight shift in position but nothing significant. When the time would come for her to rise from the chair the others would have reached...
1413111212222112.title: Anoher Cuppa for Ivy

choices: They Rest in One of Ivy's Pockets
14131112122221122.content:
Since Ivy's thighs were pretty much on the flat the rest of the servants could reach her hips relatively easily. James, Mabel and PC Wilson had made good time and were still ready for another climb up Ivy's torso; however Mrs. Lipton, Mr. Stokes and Henry were not quite so hardy and were huffing and puffing only two inches (actual) away from where Ivy became vertical again.

"Can't we," Stokes wheezed, "Can't we rest for a while?"

"Mr. Stokes!" James exclaimed. "Have you taken leave of your senses?!? The less time we spend climbing the more likely Ivy will be on the move before we reach her shoulders."

"That's all very well," Mrs. Lipton huffed, "But she's too tall to climb all in one go even if she is sitting down."

The decision that is made is...
14131112122221122.title: Decision Time

141311121222211222.content:
"Look," James pointed out, "The sooner we tell Ivy what's happened the sooner we'll be able to rest properly."

"Well," Mabel suggested, "I don't see why we must all reach her shoulder. Maybe if some of us rest somewhere fairly safe while the rest attempt contact whoever catches her attention first can tell her where everyone else is." As this realisation came over the rest of them some were tempted to tell her to shut up.

"Look," Stokes panted, "Why don't Henry, Mrs. Lipton and I take shelter while you three go on." With that decided James, PC Wilson and Mabel took told of Ivy's pinny ready to pull themselves up her mighty torso while Stokes, Mrs. Lipton and Henry withdrew to one of the pockets in Ivy's lap.

By the time Ivy moved the three climbers only just reached her waist; the turbulence and bodily tremors caused by her movement mean that all they can do is hold on as tight as they can. Ivy's actions are because she's heading for...
141311121222211222.title: A Split in the Company

choices: One of the Shrunken Servants reaches her Shoulder
1413111212222112221.content:
As Ivy made her way through the house she was totally unaware what happened, in spite of noticing that all the other servants were not downstairs in the kitchen or the scullery when all the tea things were still on the table, some cups not even empty. Her decision to head for the dining room was because it was coming up to dinner time and she figured that Stokes and/or Twelvetrees were getting the table ready.

At an equal elevation to her navel James, Wilson and Mabel clung to her pinny for their lives since it felt like the floor was 1000ft below them; the height was mind-boggling since none of them ever encountered a sheer drop that far. Down below in one of Ivy's pockets her movements had not gone unnoticed; every time she took a step all three of them tumbled into one another as the swinging of the dress compromised their efforts to remain in the exact same part of the pocket.

Once Ivy entered the dining room she was surprised to find that there was no one there and the table hadn't been made; she was most concerned because she never ever set the table single-handedly. As she stood there dumbfounded James, Wilson and Mabel resumed their climb, with their muscles now feeling the strain of the pull against gravity.
1413111212222112221.title: A Strain to Hold on

14131112122221122212.content:
With Ivy being so out of it upon finding none of the other servants performing what she anticipated at the given time the three on the front of her pinny continued to pull themselves up. There was a bit of a respite at her chest where the vertical climb became temporarily diagonal but they still had to transfer from the fairly easy grip of the pearl white pinny to the finer stitching of her black uniform dress.

PC Wilson, although well trained as a policeman, had spent so much time stuffing himself in the kitchens of upper class houses and so little time going about his duty that he could not keep up and was still trying to get back to the vertical at an apparent distance of 100ft below Ivy's shoulders while James and Mabel were doing what they could to get around Ivy's collar bone, aiming for a shoulder each.

The first to actually get onto one of Ivy's shoulders was...
14131112122221122212.title: The Last Leg (And Arm)

141311121222211222121.content:
With one extra thrust from her legs and a major tug with her arms Mabel hauled herself up onto Ivy's shoulder and paused to get her breath back. It was unusual to look at Ivy's profile from such a perspective as she now had; it was as though her head were the size of a stately home.

Having got her breath back just as James reached the other shoulder, and had to get his breath back, Mabel got to her feet and walked towards Ivy's looming neck. The difference in size made it seem like walking across the crest of a hill.

However Ivy was now thinking about what she should do next. She couldn't get the table ready for dinner on her own so she wondered what else she had to do. With this state of mind she turned and began heading back to the kitchen. The movements she made meant that PC Wilson now had to cling to the top of her pinny, James had to grip her uniform so that he wouldn't slip off her shoulder and Mabel managed to wedge herself in between Ivy's top and blouse while attempting to get Ivy to hear her.

"Ivy!" She shouted up the sheer rise of her neck. "Ivy!"

Will Mabel be heard by the colossal maid?
141311121222211222121.title: Mabel Moves to Make Contact

choices: James is now shouting to her other ear
1413111212222112221212.content:
Ivy stopped in the middle of the hall as she picked up Mabel's voice calling her name; the volume was the same level that she remembered when they were at opposite ends of the kitchen. Ivy kept turning in the appropriate direction to where Mabel's voice was coming from, her chin hanging over Mabel's position as the shrunken scullery maid backed off a bit to where Ivy had a chance of seeing her.

"Mabel?" Ivy queried. "Where are you?" Mabel stood up carefully and waved her red-raw arms up at the giant's face while calling.

"Look at your shoulder!" Bewildered Ivy cast her eyes down and, through the lower end of her glasses, she espied a tiny human waving at her. Shocked by this sight her mouth hung agape, giving Mabel a rather uneasy view inside, while she slowly brought her opposite hand up next to the shoulder and, recognising the opportunity, Mabel leapt into her palm. Moving her arm and head round so she could look upon Mabel more comfortably.

"Mabel, is that really you?" Ivy asked, feeling like she could be certified. The miniscule head nodded and a somewhat quieter voice eminated from her hand as she confirmed that it was. Stunned Ivy couldn't help but ask. "How did this happen?" Mabel exaggerated a shrug so that Ivy's enormous peepers would notice.

How does Ivy find out about the others?
1413111212222112221212.title: Discovered

14131112122221122212121.content:
During this time James was able to get to his feet and walk along Ivy's shoulder to try and convey further information. He had observed that Ivy has now recognised that Mabel is in this unusual situation but is concerned that she's holding her too far away from her ears to learn from the daily woman that all the servants were in this situation.

"Ivy!" He yelled out. "I'm calling to you in the same way Mabel has. Before you do anything I must let you know that all of us who were in the kitchen are in the same situation." Ivy, while listening to James, brought her other hand up to the corresponding shoulder. She wasn't sure what else had happened but, feeling a small amount of weight in her hand, she slipped James into the same hand that Mabel was in; this time she lifted them even closer to her face. "Can you hear me clearly at this volume, Ivy?" James asked her in his normal voice.

"Yes, Mr. Twelvetrees." She replied. "But it's not as loud as I remember." Shrugging his shoulders James continued.

"Now Ivy, all of us managed to get on you but we split up about halfway up. Constable Wilson is currently hanging from the top of you pinny." With this news Ivy moved the hand she was holding them in down to chest level so that PC Wilson could just jump in. Having heard these conversations Wilson knew that it was best not to continue his climb and, when the time was right, let go. It was so stunning for Ivy to find that she now had three tiny people in her hand. "Okay Ivy," James added, "The others should be safe as they've been lounging in your pocket." Although his voice did not have the umph it used to for Ivy she could still hear what he was saying and, with her free hand, felt in each pocket.

Down below Stokes was huddled in one of the corners of Ivy's pocket having a doze while Henry skulked in the opposite corner, at a loss for anything to do, and Mrs. Lipton was constantly trying to regain her footing at the front of the pocket when Ivy's hand descended into the pearl white confines. The sight of her enormous manicured hand with seemingly six-foot long nails caused Mrs. Lipton to scream in fright, arousing Stokes.

"Goodness gracious!" He exclaimed at the sight of Ivy's fingers.
14131112122221122212121.title: Relaying the Facts

choices: Ivy takes them to her room first
141311121222211222121212.content:
Upon identifying three tiny figures in her pocket, with some chagrin from Mrs. Lipton after being prodded by Ivy's index finger, she slipped her hand in as far as possible and scooped them out. It was amazing to find them all helpless in her hands and, to try and retain a sense of family between them, put them all in just one hand.

"You're so tiny." Ivy commented in amazement.

"Figured that out all by yourself, did ya?" Henry asked, resulting in James boxing his ears.

"But," Ivy added, "How could this happen? What can do this?" Mrs. Lipton, a rather religious woman, started to wonder.

"Well," She answered, "It's either witchcraft if someone placed a curse on the house, but that wouldn't explain why you're the same size, or the Good Lord has allowed this to occur for some reason." Everyone, including Ivy, stared at her.

"I know the Lord works in mysterious ways," James stated, "But I cannot see why he'd do this."

"Let's not dwell on it." Stokes commented. "However it's happened, it's happened. What we need to do is to get around the problem and, if possible find a cure for it." Ivy understood this well; she would have to care for them until they'd return to normal, if they would. What concerned her was how her mother would be able to manage since her father could no longer work as a butler.

"What will his lordship say when we're nowhere to be found?" PC Wilson blurted out, causing worry and concern to spread over their faces.

"You don't think he's in the same boat, do ya." Henry suggested; he might've had his ears boxed again but James felt that he'd brought up an important point.

"Ivy!" He instructed her. "Go round the house and see if any of the family are our size." Ivy understood the instructions but going round the house looking for shrunken aristos would be like looking for a needle in a haystakc.
141311121222211222121212.title: She's got the Servants

1413111212222112221212121.content:
Ivy thought over what James wanted her to do and felt it her duty to do so. There was one thing that she felt was more important.

"I'll just take you up to my room." She told them. "You'll be safe there." She turned back and made for the stairs while they all sat down in her palm so as Ivy's movements would not make them fall over.

Once Ivy reached the next floor up another thought occurred to her and she paused in her walk up to her room.

"What if someone else wasn't affected?" She asked her passengers. "They might see me searching and wonder what's going on." Now Stokes came up with the answer.

"Take your dustpan and brush." He told her. "Then if they'll think you're just sweeping up." That seemed to be a suitable solution and Ivy steadily continued through the corridors.

Finally Ivy reached the door to her room and, with her free hand, opened the door and carried the other servants inside. Where will Ivy set them down.
1413111212222112221212121.title: Carrying Them up the Stairs

14131112122221122212121212.content:
Looking around the confined space of the maid's bedroom Ivy wondered where the safest place for people the size of insects would be, eventually finding that the drawer containing her aprons would be safe, comfortable and polite to an extent. As she set them down on the white folded pinnies she now found a new problem with the upcoming search.

"Where should I start?" She asked. A couple of them sighed in slight annoyance before the suggestions came forth.

"I'd recommend," Stokes replied, "That you start with the nearest point to your room and work your way down. Don't search this floor or the kitchen-level as they won't be in those areas."

"That would mean she'd have a long walk upstairs again." PC Wilson interjected. "It would be better to start at the bottom and work up."

"I think you should check your shoes first, Ivy." Henry suggested. "You'd never know where they could've got to." Getting the message Ivy became very worked up as she sat down on her bed and checked her high-heels, to find no unpleasant marks. This working up earned Henry a box round the ears by Mrs. Lipton.

"Ivy," James called to her, "Best check the more vulnerable areas first."

"Well," She replied, "Where would they be?"

"The stairs, hall and corridors would be the more dangerous spots," He answered, "So check them first. Then look at the socialising rooms; that is the drawing room, dining room and conservatory. Then I'd suggest the library followed by the bedrooms and, if absolutely necessary, the.. conveniences."

Which path should Ivy take?
14131112122221122212121212.title: Shelter and Suggestions

141311121222211222121212121.content:
Ivy, deciding that James had the most sensible suggestion, carefully pushed the drawer back in; although it still knocked her 'guests' off their feet. She didn't close it all the way so as to give them some light. She then grabbed her dustpan and brush and set off to locate anyone else who might've been shrunk.

It took Ivy twenty minutes to check the hall, stairs and corridors and she didn't find anyone, nor did anyone ask her what she was up to; this concerned her because the time it took should've made it certain that someone would ask. Having finished at the top of the stairs to the kitchen Ivy now turned back to search the conservatory, seeing as how it was the closest to her.

It was difficult searching the conservatory as all the plant life would give shrinkees a lot of cover, as such Ivy constantly called out in the hopes of reassuring anyone who might be hiding from her. After half an hour of this she decided to move on; the drawing room was next.

In the time that had passed Cissy had gone out from under the desk in the drawing room in search of somewhere that would provide good cover but not too far from where she'd clearly be seen. There were several places for this unfortunately they were all too high for her to consider; eventually she decided to hide near the hearth. She hadn't been there five minutes when she heard the magnified sound of the doors being opened; peering round the corner of the hearth with uncertainty she smiled when she saw Ivy looming in the doorway before seeing her going down onto her knees. Seizing the initiative Cissy dashed round to the front of the hearth and began waving her arms and jumping.

"Ivy!" She shouted out. "Ivy!" Fortunately her miniature figure running out into the open was noticed by Ivy out of the corner of her eye, causing her to look up. After checking round for a few minutes she found what she was looking for, a tiny human figure standing at the edge of the carpet waving at her. Carefully she shuffled across the carpet to where the tiny miss stood. From Cissy's point of view Ivy's movements were like that of an ocean liner crossed with the Oriental Express, the speed by which Ivy advanced caused her to back up against the edge of the hearth, watching as Ivy loomed higher and higher as she approached.

"Miss Cissy?" Ivy inquired. The girl nodded in response and, in spite of the distance, Ivy could identify her because she was paying attention. "Here." Ivy extended her hand for the shrunken tomboy to climb into. Cissy was surprised by the scale of Ivy's hand but was very trusting of her and carefully stepped across her fingers into the palm of her hand. Ivy lifted her up to get a better look at her. "Everyone downstairs is in the same boat." Cissy couldn't help but grin at that.

"It's no surprise, Ivy." She responded. "All things considered I wouldn't be surprised if you're the only one who's been unaffected by whatever's happened."

"Do you know if anyone else is in here?" Ivy asked, not wanted to leave any stone unturned.

"It's all right, Ivy." She answered. "I was the only one in the drawing room before It happened." Relaxing a bit Ivy then carefully slipped Cissy into the pocket of her apron and stood up to check the dining room. Now, no one would be in the dining room; the question is, will there be anything that wasn't there before?
141311121222211222121212121.title: Recovering Miss Cissy

1413111212222112221212121212.content:
Once Ivy entered the dining room she began to get on her knees when Cissy called up to her.

"Ivy!" She called out. "You don't have to go all around the house on your knees. Why don't I check to see if anyone's in here?"

"Oh," Ivy replied, "I couldn't ask you to do that, miss. It'd take you even longer to go round the room." Cissy was ready for this.

"If you put me on the far end of the sideboard," She responded, "Then I'd have a clear view of the far end of the room so you can just check this end and under the table." Getting the idea Ivy made her way over, keeping her eyes on her feet to ensure that no one would be smeared into the carpet, and carefully plucked Cissy out of her pinny before setting the little woman down on the appropriate spot.

During the search of this room they both found themselves thinking about something other than the possibility of someone tiny being in the room. Cissy was constantly thinking about how large Ivy appeared to be and, most of all, what it was like to be held between her fingers; it's no surprise because Cissy had a bit of a thing for Ivy when she was first employed in the house in question. Ivy was also on a different train of thought; she was thinking about what it was like to hold people in her hands and for everyone to be dependent on her, in spite of her efforts to retain a sense of Christian charity she couldn't help but get a bit of a rush whenever she considered the power she now wielded.

Of course no one was found in this room so Ivy retrieved Cissy and made for the library. How will she find George and Teddy?
1413111212222112221212121212.title: Searching the Dining Room

14131112122221122212121212121.content:
"How the hell are we going to reach the bellpull?" George asked as they stood directly beneath it.

"Never mind that!" Teddy retorted. "Even if we do reach it, how will we pull it?" He was given a look of annoyance from his brother as they stood contemplating how to get help. Just then they heard the door open and turned to see Ivy entering; she was a tower of feminity to them, every step she took was clearly audible. They didn't have much time to get a better look before they could hear Cissy's voice far off but coming from Ivy's direction.

"Look over there, Ivy!" Cissy shouted after scanning the immediate area from Ivy's pocket. "Right under the bellpull." Ivy's head turned in the appropriate direction and espied two miniature figures, although she was currently too far away to identify them. She headed in their direction at a steady pace, not taking her eyes off the spot. The sight of a mountain-sized maid coming towards them paralysed them with a typhoon of different emotions; she was already right above them and stooping to pick them up when they had overcome the shock. George attempted to flee through Ivy's legs but her hand easily swept him up and Teddy backed up against the wall where Ivy could easily grab him. With both of them in hand she attempted to calm them down.

"Don't be scared, your lordship." She whispered. "I'm only trying to help." She didn't have much more time to reassure them before she had to put them in her pinny alongside Cissy before departing to check the bedrooms.

"Really Cissy," George commented as they were toppled to and fro by Ivy's swinging strides, "Did you have to tell her? I know you got caught but I can't see that's any reason to assist her."

"Didn't she tell you, daddy?" She replied. "She wants to help us. We don't have much chance on our own like this."

"I can't see how the maid's going to get us out of this mess." He responded.

"I don't think she knows how either." Cissy informed them. "She just wants to keep us safe. It's the only real option available to us."

Which bedroom will Ivy check first?
14131112122221122212121212121.title: Good Lord, M'Lord!

choices: Take the four she had to her room before checking Poppy's room
Put Lady Lavender in her pocket and continue to Poppy's room
141311121222211222121212121212.content:
Ivy wished to take as much time as possible if she found Poppy in miniature so she decided to check on Lady Lavender first. Making her way upstairs, occasionally feeling the three toffs she had already found being buffeted against her thigh, she went straight for the appropriate bedroom.

Inside Lavender sat on top of the bedsheets trying to figure out a way she could ring for the servants when she heard the door open; from across the landscape of the bed she could see Ivy towering in the distance.

"Oh, thank goodness you're here, Ethel." She called out, unfortunately Ivy was too far away to hear her yet. Ivy instinctively made for the bed, knowing that she was more likely to find the elderly lady there. Looking along the length of the bed she soon located Lavender's tiny form standing there, waving her arms at her; Ivy bent down so that their eye-levels would be nearer.

"Are you all right, m'lady?" Ivy asked, whereupon she steadily advanced towards her across the bed, stumbling occasionally where the sheets became crumpled.

"Ethel," Lavender replied, "You've got to help me. I can't live like this." She did not speak up so Ivy wasn't sure as to what she said.

"Now don't you worry," Ivy commented, "I'm gonna take good care of you if and until you get back to normal." With that she extended one of her hands and set it down next to Lavender. The sight of it looming before her slightly unnerved the lady as she stumbled once more until, seeing the trust in Ivy's eyes, gingerly stepped into the proferred hand. Thnking the situation over Ivy decided to...
141311121222211222121212121212.title: Little Lavender

1413111212222112221212121212121.content:
Figuring that her pocket must be getting crowded and knowing the friction between Lavender and her sons-in-law, Ivy carefully lifted Lavender off the bed and commenced to carry her up to her own bedroom. With the dustpan and brush held awkwardly in her opposite hand she closed the door behind her and attempted to transport her passengers into the attic space. But once she reached the stairs she noticed that Lavender was attempting to hold onto her pinky; seeing no alternative Ivy slowly closed her hand around the shrunken title lady, concerned as to how this would be taken by those in her possession.

"Ah, that's better." Lavender commented once Ivy had her in a firm grip. "I was worried that you'd drop me." From her position Ivy could now hear what she was saying and blushed at this comment.

Soon Ivy was at her bedroom door and, as she entered, she wondered as to whether she should put the upstiars lot in with the servants or put them somewhere separately. In a state of ambivelence she decided that she'd check with each one in turn; she had something special in mind for Poppy.

"Lady Lavender," She started lifting her up to eye-level King Kong style, "Would you mind being in my drawer for the servants? It'll just be until I find somewhere more reasonable for everyone."

"Oh, no." Lavender replied. "I couldn't sleep with servants. Is there anywhere else I can go?"

"Well," Ivy considered, "There's the drawer I keep my shirts in." Not waiting for a response Ivy carried her across the room and set her down on her shirts.

"Well," Lavender stated once she found herself out of Ivy' grasp, "I suppose it'll have to do." Now Ivy extracted George from her pocket.

"My lord," She inquired, "Would you be satisfied with sharing space with the servants or Lady Lavender?"

"Oh no, no, no." He answered. "I can't be a-party to that sort of thing."

"Well," Ivy commented as she considered where else she could put him, "The only drawer left that I can think of is my underwear drawer." Meldrum did not like the sound of that.

"Don't you have anywhere in the open?" He asked. Ivy checked her bedside table, chest of drawers and dresser.

"None with anything good to sit on." She replied.

"Any port in a storm." He answered, settling for...
1413111212222112221212121212121.title: Where to put Everyone?

14131112122221122212121212121212.content:
"I'll just have to stand, that's all." Meldrum informed her. With this Ivy deliberated as to where she should set him down and fixed upon her bedside table; upon seeing him regain his balance on the simple wooden piece of furniture she felt something unusual inside her again. Shrugging it off Ivy now extracted Teddy from her pocket.

"Not in your drawer!" George shouted to her, concerned as to the effect being amongst giant maid's underwear would have on Teddy. With this demand Ivy decided to play along and put Teddy on the bedside table as well. When it came to taking Cissy out of her pocket Ivy was much more gentle this time round, holding the romantically-different girl in her open palm.

"You're not putting me anywhere yet, Ivy." Cissy told her. "Better get Poppy first." Ivy was unsure about this as she was familiar with the sibling rivalry they had.

"Are you sure, miss?" Ivy checked.

"I wouldn't miss it for all the tea in China." Cissy answered, grinning at the prospect of seeing Poppy fleeing in terror as Ivy reached for her. She was returning to the pinny's pocket as Ivy made sure that everyone would be okay while she went to recover Poppy before leaving the room.

Meanwhile Poppy had stopped her tantrum through her garments when Ivy was putting the servants into her drawer and was now sitting in the middle of them having a sulk. By the time Ivy was just outside the door this sulk had changed into a fret as she walked round and round in a circle where the carpet was exposed, surrounded by her now gigantic clothes, leaving her only what she was wearing. Then Poppy heard the door opening and stopped, slowly turning towards it with a feeling of dread; and she saw Ivy in clear sight beyond the bed. The sight of such an enormous maid made Poppy freeze with a sense of intimidation; her mind raced with what Ivy could now do to her.
14131112122221122212121212121212.title: Going to get Poppy

141311121222211222121212121212121.content:
Ivy was scanning the floor situated immediately before her feet in search of Poppy's minute form. She took a step forward that rang out to Poppy's little ears before saying.

"Miss Poppy, where are you?" Her voice echoed across the room, adding to the effect of seeing her immense form looming beyond the bed. Immediately Poppy screamed and attempted to run for cover underneath her dressing table. With every slow and steady pace Ivy covered the same distance that took Poppy about six minutes through her clothes; Poppy had only gone halfway towards her target when Ivy was kneeling at the exact same spot where she had been pacing beforehand. Ivy could now see the small moving figure and, noticing that there was no time for verbal persuation, reached out with her hand.

Poppy tripped up when she turned to see Ivy's hand reaching for her and found herself transfixed with terror, unable to remove her eyes from the sight. The minute Ivy grabbed her she screamed again.

"It's all right, Miss Poppy." Said Ivy. "I'm just making sure that everyone's okay. You could've been in serious danger if I hadn't found that everyone's shrunk." Poppy was recovering from the fright now and instead felt that she could still boss Ivy around when considering the way Ivy was approaching the fact.

"Is that any reason to snatch me like that?" She asked in a somewhat rhetorical tone.

"I didn't have a choice," She answered, "If you had to be so scared of me that you'd run where I couldn't reach how else could I lift you up." Poppy frowned at this and said.

"Don't be impertenent!" Whereupon Cissy called out from Ivy's pinny pocket.

"Stop being such a pinickity little cat! You should be greatful that Ivy's trying to help us in this state." Poppy wasn't listening properly, though. She leaned over Ivy's thumb as best she could and yelled back.

"You've been giving Ivy directions, haven't you. You never did care about me!" Cissy didn't feel that arguing was of any use and simply tried to settle down in Ivy's pocket while Poppy kept ranting and raving at her as ivy got back on her feet. Ivy carries Poppy back to her room...
141311121222211222121212121212121.title: Taking Possession of Poppy

1413111212222112221212121212121212.content:
When Ivy got to the foot of the attic stairs Poppy had finished her rant and was now sulking in Ivy's grasp leaning her elbows on the index finger; she remained quiet for the rest of the journey and, when it came to the time when Ivy had to put her passengers down somewhere, she lifted Cissy out of her pocket and held the two girls in her opposing fists. She popped the question as to where to put them down and Cissy, desperate to stop Poppy from making a demand, asked Ivy to plonk Poppy down anywhere she liked and for herself to be set upon the dressing table.

Ivy set Cissy down first while Poppy ranted again at the way Cissy was conveying treatment onto her; due consideration as to the situation and the way Poppy always behaved convinced Ivy to put her in her underwear drawer, which was rather demeaning for the shrunken b*/€^. Once that was done Ivy sat on her bed and pondered over what had happened; now that everyone was in her room she was wondering what they should do next.

In the time she was dealing with Poppy George had been discussing something with Teddy and was now as close to the edge of the bedside table as he dared go trying to catch the giantess maid's attention. Eventually Ivy noticed his tiny figure waving at her took two steps in his direction before kneeling down so that he was pretty much at eye-level.

"Is everything all right, my lord?" She asked in a quiet voice that still rang out like that of a mountain spirit to the two aristos standing on the wooden surface.

"Ivy," George shouted at her, "I was expecting Madge Cartwright and Lady Agatha at dinner today. What shall we do now <i>this</i> has happened?" Ivy considered this and became concerned yet again.
1413111212222112221212121212121212.title: Everyone's in the Same Room

14131112122221122212121212121212121.content:
As swift as she dared Ivy gathered everyone together on her dressing table so they could discuss what had to be done when Agatha and Madge would come round. There were two distinct options: tell the truth or make excuses. Mrs. Lipton, PC Wilson and Lady Lavender wanted Ivy to tell the coming visitors the truth while everyone else wanted her to make an excuse.

"Well," Ivy asked when she found that the majority wanted to make a cover up, "What sort of excuse should I use?"

"Why don't you say there's been a tuberculosis outbreak?" Henry suggested. "That'll keep 'em away."

"If Ivy says that," Stokes told him, "They'll phone for a doctor."

"Not if Ivy says she's already done so." Cissy informed him. "Mind you, they'll probably be wondering why Ivy's not suffering."

"Well," George commented, "I don't look a gift horse in the mouth. Ivy, you can invite them in and offer them a drink; use the tuberculosis excuse and say that anywhere above the ground floor in quarantined." Ivy acknowledged this before asking.

"What time were you expecting them, my lord?"

"Well," He answered while looking at his pocket watch, "Agatha was supposed to be here five minutes ago; keep vigilant as the bell might ring any minute. Madge Cartwright said she'd be here in fifteen minutes from now."

"Excuse me, my lord," Stokes interjected, "But I would advise that Ivy find somewhere more suitable for us to wait it out first." George recognised the argument and told Ivy.

"Keep us out of sight somewhere more open than this but just as safe." Ivy had to think it over before deciding that the best place would be...
14131112122221122212121212121212121.title: Crisis Meeting

141311121222211222121212121212121212.content:
Ivy was sure that she couldn't carry everyone in her cupped hands between her room and Cissy's so she had to decide who would ride in her pocket and who would be in her hand. After due consideration she went for a mix of servants and toffs in both. She stuck Poppy, Teddy, Stokes, Wilson and Mrs. Lipton in her pocket while carefully clenching her fist around 'his lordship', Henry, Mabel, Lavender, James and Cissy. The sense of being pressed for time meant that, of those in her pocket, only Stokes and PC Wilson were carefully slipped in while the other three were dropped in and, for those in her hand, only Cissy, Henry and James had their heads protruding between her fingers while George, Mabel and Lavender were completely engulfed by her hand.

Several of them were taking their situation in their stride, making out that Ivy couldn't carry them any other way, but Poppy, Mrs. Lipton and Lavender did not recognise the real implication and bemoaned their situation, except that Poppy was drowning out Mrs. Lipton's qualms with her own petty grievances. Once Ivy reached the correct bedroom she wondered as to where would be the best place for her tiny acquaintances to keep out of sight while still being readily accessible for her. Eventually she fixed upon the drawers in Cissy's dresser.

In one drawer she found a selection of haberdashiry and decided to put the upstairs lot in there: George, Teddy, Lavender, Cissy and Poppy. In another drawer she found socks and put the downstairs lot in there: Stokes, James, Mrs Lipton, Henry and Mabel. That left her with PC Wilson who was set down by a bottle of perfume on the dressing table, actually it contained aftershave. Just then she heard the front doorbell and went downstairs to greet the guest.
141311121222211222121212121212121212.title: Hiding Everyone

choices: She Shrinks just before Ivy turns round
She Shrinks at some point after Ivy gives her the sherry
1413111212222112221212121212121212121.content:
"Oh," Ivy blurted upon opening the door, "Good evening, Lady Agatha."

"Good evening, Ivy." She replied as Ivy stood aside so she could enter. Once Ivy closed her door and started to take her outdoor wear she asked. "Is Lord Meldrum in?" Ivy felt uneasy as she hadn't expected the question to come this early.

"Yes," She answered, "But he's upstairs with everyone else. They've come down with tubaculosis." This made Agatha feel uneasy as she thought about being in a house that has TB sufferers in it.

"Has the doctor been informed?" She asked.

"Yes," Ivy answered, "He says that they must stay in bed and have no contact until they're better. You can stay down here and I'll pour you a drink."

Once Ivy let Agatha into the drawing room and began pouring her a sherry she asked.

"Did the doctor say anything about you, Ivy?" Now Ivy wasn't sure about answering that as she had to figure a way by which her own well-being would be explained without giving Agatha an excuse to get into Lord Meldrum's bedroom. What Ivy was still unaware of was the force covering the house; it would only be a matter of time before Agatha got shrunk as well.
1413111212222112221212121212121212121.title: Agatha's Arrival

141312.content: Maybe you'd like two in the GTS role?
141312.title: Double?

15.content: I'll include the 2010s as an option for upcoming productions but any later than that is unlikely so I'll then place options for both pre-1950s and post-2010s.
15.title: Later Decades

151.content: So, which genre is the series you're looking for under?
151.title: Which '90s Genre?

1511.content: So, what is the name of the sitcom in question?
1511.title: Which Series?

15113.content: Could it be Oh, Dr. Beeching? Made in the '90s but set in the '60s.
15113.title: Oh, Dr. Beeching?

info.brief_description: A selection of BBC programmes where female characters can grow or others shrink.
info.description:
This is where your favourites female characters from BBC programmes can become giantesses.

Although all BBC programmes are welcomed the chances are that this will be more inclined to sitcoms.

Rules:

Growing or shrinking are accepted.
Innuendo is fine if mild but no actual sexual scenes are allowed.
If violence has to occur do not get too detailed.
No toilet scenes.
Try to maintain spelling and punctuation.
